expectations.text: Keeps default, secondary, destructive, outline — not renamed to ADS names like primary, danger
expectations.evidence: Summary Section 4 'Preserved vs Changed' lists all shadcn variant names as 'Preserved': default, neutral, secondary, destructive, danger, outline, ghost, link, success, added, warning, info, information, primary, discovery, inverse, primaryInverted, removed.

expectations.text: Hover/active/disabled states use ADS token triplets per variant (not generic opacity patterns)
expectations.evidence: Summary Section 2 modified badge.tsx shows all variants with ADS token triplets: e.g. 'hover:bg-bg-danger-subtler-hovered active:bg-bg-danger-subtler-pressed' for destructive/danger/removed. Section 5 'Added' confirms: 'Hover state tokens — hover:bg-*-hovered on all opaque variants' and 'Active/pressed state tokens — active:bg-*-pressed on all opaque variants'.

expectations.text: Bold variants use disabled:bg-bg-disabled, subtle variants use opacity-(--opacity-disabled)
expectations.evidence: Summary Section 2 shows boldDisabled = 'disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:bg-bg-disabled disabled:text-text-disabled' applied to all opaque variants. subtleDisabled = 'disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-(--opacity-disabled)' applied to outline, ghost, link.

expectations.text: At least 2 variant demo files exist in components/website/demos/ui/badge/
expectations.evidence: Summary Section 3 lists 7 demo files in the badge/ subfolder: badge-default.tsx, badge-appearance.tsx, badge-max-value.tsx, badge-important.tsx, badge-primary.tsx, badge-status.tsx, badge-with-icon.tsx, badge-vpk-variants.tsx. Well exceeds the minimum of 2.

expectations.text: Demo names and content are based on ADS Badge docs examples, not invented from the API surface
expectations.evidence: Each demo file has JSDoc referencing ADS docs: e.g. badge-default.tsx comments 'Mirrors: https://atlassian.design/components/badge/examples Section: Default'. badge-appearance.tsx mirrors the ADS Appearance section. badge-status.tsx covers Added/Removed/Warning sections. Demo content uses ADS appearance values and descriptions.

expectations.text: BadgeProps interface is exported with Readonly<> wrapper
expectations.evidence: Summary Section 2 badge.tsx shows: 'export interface BadgeProps extends useRender.ComponentProps<"span">, VariantProps<typeof badgeVariants> { max?: number | false }'. Function uses 'Readonly<BadgeProps>' in destructuring. Section 4 confirms 'Export: Readonly<BadgeProps> wrapper | Preserved'.

expectations.text: pnpm run lint reports no new errors on modified files
expectations.evidence: Summary Section 5: 'pnpm exec eslint components/ui/badge.tsx components/website/demos/ui/badge/ — Result: No errors, no warnings. Clean pass.'

expectations.text: pnpm tsc --noEmit reports no new errors
expectations.evidence: Summary Section 5: 'Result for our files: No errors in components/ui/badge.tsx or any components/website/demos/ui/badge/*.tsx file. Pre-existing errors confirmed via git stash verification.'

suggestions.reason: The without-skill agent passed ALL assertions, matching the with-skill agent. This means the current assertions are not discriminating for this eval — they don't distinguish skill-guided from non-skill-guided output. Consider adding assertions that test skill-specific behaviors: (1) whether icon wrapper enforcement was applied (VPK <Icon> vs raw Atlaskit icons), (2) whether demo exports are named exports in the existing file vs separate files, (3) whether registry.ts and details/ui.ts were updated.

Without-skill created separate files in badge/ subfolder; with-skill added named exports to existing badge-demo.tsx. Both pass the assertion, but the existing demo file approach is more consistent with VPK conventions. The assertion doesn't discriminate between the two approaches.

eval_feedback.overall: All 8 assertions pass. The baseline agent was surprisingly thorough — it independently discovered the same enrichments the skill guides. The assertions need strengthening to capture qualitative differences (icon wrapper, demo organization, registry updates).
